twitter_thread: 2300 Neocities sites gone in an instant: Namecheap's mass takedown without warning. Still using them?
Transfer your domain in 30 minutes with dns-api or transfer-domain, like 500 sites already have
Neocities' single-point failure on Namecheap DNS cost them 100% uptime. Use Cloudflare's distributed DNS to avoid this
Buried in Namecheap's terms: 'We can revoke your domain at any time'. Know your registrar's fine print
Cloudflare Registrar and Google Domains offer 24/7 support and transparent dispute resolution. Worth the switch?
1 hour to migrate your domain vs potential permanent loss: which sounds better?
What registrar do you use and why? #indieweb #domains
pack.linkedin: Namecheap's sudden takedown of 2300 Neocities sites exposes a critical vulnerability: relying on a single registrar. To mitigate this risk, use scripts like dns-api or transfer-domain to transfer your domain within 30 minutes, as 500 sites have already done. Consider Cloudflare's distributed DNS to ensure 100% uptime. Review your registrar's terms of service, as Namecheap's contract allows them to revoke domains at will. Alternatives like Cloudflare Registrar and Google Domains provide transparent policies and 24/7 support. How will you protect your online presence from similar disruptions?
